{"step_by_step_thinking": "The query is about the causative agent of pregnancy gingivitis. Reviewing the provided documents, Document [3] (Title: Gingivitis -- Epidemiology) states that 'Gingivitis is more prevalent in pregnant women than in non-pregnant women. Moreover, more severe forms of gingivitis have been more often seen in pregnant women.' Document [9] (Title: Gingivitis -- Etiology) provides more details, stating that 'During pregnancy, there are not only changes in hormone levels but also a greater predisposition to dilating blood vessels. These factors contribute to an exaggerated inflammatory response by the gingival tissues, even to a minor quantity of plaque accumulation.' This suggests that the bacteria causing gingivitis in pregnant women are likely to be those associated with plaque accumulation. Among the options, Fusobacterium (Option D) is mentioned in Document [8] (Title: Gingivitis -- Etiology) as one of the microorganisms more strongly associated with the etiology of gingivitis. Therefore, the most likely causative agent of pregnancy gingivitis is Fusobacterium.", "answer_choice": "D"}
